articles.title: An explosion at a fireworks plant in China kills at least 26 people, state media says
articles.summary: Authorities said search and rescue at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has largely been completed, but verification of the casualties and identification of the victims are still underwa

articles.title: These 7 Primary Elections Will Test President Trump’s Power in Indiana
articles.summary: Indiana voters were deciding whether to support Republican state lawmakers who rejected the president’s push to redraw the state’s political map last year. Mr. Trump backed challengers.

articles.title: Scott Turow's latest real-life legal thriller: Suing Meta for copyright infringement
articles.summary: Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.

articles.title: Cruise ship with hantavirus may have seen a rare occurrence: humans infecting humans
articles.summary: Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.

articles.title: Trump Will Control His Image at Palm Beach’s Newly Named Trump Airport
articles.summary: The Trump family won’t profit from branded merchandise sold at the airport. But it will have control over any biographical material presented about President Trump there.

articles.title: U.S. says it's made Hormuz safe for commercial shipping, but standoff hasn't changed
articles.summary: The administration has announced a new military strategy designed to open up the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping, but it's unclear if this will break Iran's grip on the key waterway.

articles.title: Trump Looks for a Silver Bullet to End the Iran War. There May Be None.
articles.summary: The president is trying to ratchet up the economic pressure on Tehran, but Iran’s government is unlikely to make a deal without a big, face-saving compromise.

articles.title: Democrats Urge N.Y. Leaders to Redistrict After Supreme Court Ruling
articles.summary: The House minority leader, Hakeem Jeffries, sent a familiar emissary to the New York State Capitol to convey the party’s urgency to draw more favorable maps.

articles.title: Midwives saved his mom's life -- and inspired him to pursue the profession
articles.summary: May 5 is International Day of the Midwife. This year's theme is "one million more" — reflecting a shortage of midwives.

articles.title: NPR went looking for Polymarket's Panama headquarters. It's elusive
articles.summary: The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.

articles.title: F.D.A. Blocked Publication of Research Finding Covid and Shingles Vaccines Were Safe
articles.summary: The agency’s scientists and data contractors reviewed millions of patient records for studies that were pulled back before release.

articles.title: A legal scholar and 'Backtalker' defends critical race theory -- a term she helped coin
articles.summary: Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw helped name two contested ideas in U.S. politics — intersectionality and critical race theory. Her memoir chronicles the personal and legal framework for her thinking.

articles.title: The Iran war sent jet fuel prices sky-high. Here's what air travelers should know
articles.summary: Fuel is airlines' second-largest expense, and getting even costlier due to the U.S. war with Iran. Airlines are looking for ways to cope, starting by hiking checked baggage fees for most fliers.

articles.title: A nearly year-long deployment at sea takes a toll on military families
articles.summary: Families in Norfolk, Va., await the return of roughly 15,000 sailors and Marines as the USS Ford continues a deployment that started more than 10 months ago.

articles.title: Did FBI Director Kash Patel use AI to rip off the Beastie Boys?
articles.summary: An analysis by NPR suggests that frames from an iconic music video were used to generate content for a tweet by the FBI director.

articles.title: What We Know About ‘Project Freedom’ in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Violence flared in the waterway on Monday after President Trump said the United States would help guide stranded ships. It’s not clear exactly how the plan is working.

articles.title: Paxton’s Fund-Raising Struggles in Texas Underscore Deep Rift in G.O.P.
articles.summary: The Texas attorney general is heading into the runoff against Senator John Cornyn without the support of some of his longtime backers.

articles.title: SCOTUS temporarily restores mifepristone access. And, Indiana, Ohio vote in primaries
articles.summary: The Supreme Court temporarily restored access to the abortion pill mifepristone. And, Indiana's primaries will test President Trump's influence as he tries to oust incumbent Republican senators.

articles.title: Canadian prime minister says Europe will anchor world order
articles.summary: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney tells European leaders they, not the U.S., will anchor the world order in the future.

articles.title: U.S. says the Iran ceasefire holds despite attacks in Strait of Hormuz and against UAE
articles.summary: So far, only two merchant ships are known to have passed a new U.S.-guarded route through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Ohio’s 2026 Senate Primary Election Kicks Off With Attacks About Epstein
articles.summary: Ohio’s unusual contest — with a little-known incumbent and a well-known challenger — shows how Democrats are hoping to capitalize on G.O.P. voters’ anger at the Jeffrey Epstein scandal.

articles.title: Cruise ship waiting for help after 3 people died in a suspected hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean after three passengers died in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
